name: Salmon in Pesto Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Salmon fillet, cut into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine lemon juice, rice vinegar, 2 tablespoons light soy sauce, sugar, salt, Sichuan peppercorns, and black pepper. Set aside.
2. In a separate bowl, combine the remaining tablespoon soy sauce, sherry, sesame oil, and ginger. Rub the fish with this mixture and marinate for at least 20 minutes. This can be done several hours in advance.
3. Fill a large pan with water and bring to a rapid boil. Meanwhile, place a layer of foil in a Chinese steamer tray, covering it except for about 1 inch around the edges to allow steam to circulate. Rub the foil with a little peanut oil.
4. Lay the salmon on the foil in the tray and place the tray over the boiling water. Cover and steam the salmon until light pink, firm to the touch, and flakes easily when tested with a fork. (A 1/2-inch-thick fillet should take about 5 minutes.)
5. In a small bowl, combine cornstarch with 1 tablespoon cold water.
6. Place a sauté pan over medium-high heat. Add 1 tablespoon peanut oil. When hot, add the remaining ginger and garlic. Sauté for 15 seconds, then add the lemon juice mixture. Bring to a slow boil, stir in scallions and cilantro, and then stir in the small amount of cornstarch mixture to lightly thicken the sauce. Remove from heat.
7. When the salmon is cooked, remove the steamer tray and lift the fish out on foil. Slide the fish onto a platter or dinner plates. Spoon the sauce over the fish. Serve immediately.
8. Serves 2 as a main course, 6 as part of a multi-course Asian dinner.
9. NOTE: Salmon is excellent served at room temperature or eaten cold while you stand in front of the refrigerator. The pesto sauce is good on any steamed, baked, broiled, or grilled firm-fleshed fish fillet.
